paper.title: Gaia DR2 reveals a very massive runaway star ejected from R136

extraction.summary: No Galactic-unbound HVS candidates. Paper studies two very massive O2 stars (VFTS 16 and VFTS 72, each ~100 Msun) in the Tarantula Nebula of the Large Magellanic Cloud (LMC). Both are found to be fast runaway stars with space velocities ~100 km/s relative to R136 and the local massive star population. VFTS 16 is consistent with dynamical ejection from R136 ~1.5 Myr ago. These are LMC-internal cluster runaway stars, not Galactic-unbound objects. The paper never claims Galactic unbound status or hypervelocity classification.

method_chain.summary: Gaia DR2 catalog sources within 0.2 deg of R136, screened to G<17, cross-matched with VFTS O-type stars.

source_refs.context: Gaia DR2 extraction within 0.2 deg of R136, G<17, cross-match with VFTS.

method_chain.summary: Filter out foreground stars via parallax and radial velocity. Exclude sources with proper motion errors > 0.1 mas/yr. Further restrict to 79 O-stars with errors < 0.07 mas/yr.

source_refs.context: Proper motion filtering: pm_error < 0.07 mas/yr, resulting in 79 O-stars.

method_chain.summary: Convert relative proper motions to tangential velocities assuming LMC distance 50.1±2.5 kpc. Compute proper motion relative to R136 central stars. Derive flight times to R136.
outputs: VFTS 16: 80±11 km/s; VFTS 72: 93±15 km/s tangential. 3D: 112, 93 km/s.

source_refs.context: Table 1: properties including tangential and 3D speeds.

method_chain.summary: Assess dynamical ejection from R136. VFTS 16 position angle consistent with R136 origin; flight time ~1.5 Myr. VFTS 72 ~2σ discrepant in position angle. Both classified as runaway stars from R136 region.
outputs: VFTS 16: consistent with R136 ejection. VFTS 72: less clear origin. Both are runaways.

source_refs.context: Discussion and Conclusions: runaway classification, ejection from R136.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Two very massive O2 stars in 30 Doradus/LMC: VFTS 16 (O2 III, ~100 Msun) and VFTS 72 (BI 253, O2 III-V(n)((f*)), ~100 Msun). Both have proper motions exceeding 0.3 mas/yr relative to R136.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: These stars are LMC-internal cluster runaway stars from R136, not Galactic-unbound HVS candidates. Their space velocities are ~100 km/s relative to R136 — typical of cluster escapers, far below Galactic escape velocity (~500+ km/s). Paper uses 'runaway star' terminology, never claims unbound from Galaxy or hypervelocity status. The paper focuses on cluster ejection dynamics (DES), not Galactic escape.

source_refs.context: Abstract: Both stars are 'fast runaway stars, with space velocities of around 100 km/s relative to R136 and the local massive star population'.
Conclusions: VFTS 16 ejected from R136 by dynamical interaction. VFTS 72 also a proper motion runaway.
